passive.name: King's Tribute
passive.description: When an enemy unit dies near Trundle, he heals for a percent of its maximum Health.

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} Enemies that die near {{ci|Trundle}} cause him to {{tip|heal}} based on {{as|their '''maximum''' health}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Innate:}} Whenever a nearby enemy dies, '''Trundle''' {{tip|heal|heals}} himself for {{as|{{pp|key=%|1.8 to 5.5}} of the target's '''maximum''' health}}.
wiki_detail.notes: * ''King's Tribute'' does not trigger when an enemy structure is destroyed.

Q.name: Chomp
Q.description: Trundle bites his opponent, dealing damage, briefly slowing and sapping some of their Attack Damage.
Q.tooltip: Trundle's next Attack deals {{ totaldamage }} physical damage and briefly Slows by {{ slowamount*100 }}%, then Trundle gains {{ bonusad }} Attack Damage for {{ sapdebuffduration }} seconds and the enemy loses {{ sappedad*-1 }} Attack Damage for the same duration.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{cis|Trundle}} next basic attack with a few seconds will deal {{as|'''bonus''' physical damage}} and briefly {{tip|slow}} the target.
wiki_detail.blurb2: Afterwards, '''Trundle''' gains {{sti|{{as|'''bonus''' attack damage}}}} for a few seconds, during which he reduces the target's {{as|attack damage}}.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Trundle''' empowers his next [[basic attack]] within 7 seconds to have an {{tip|uncancellable windup}}, gain {{sti|25 '''bonus''' range}}, deal {{as|'''bonus''' physical damage}} and {{tip|slow}} the target by 75% for {{fd|0.1}} seconds.
wiki_detail.description2: After using the empowered attack, '''Trundle''' gains {{sti|{{as|'''bonus''' attack damage}}}} for 5 seconds and reduces the target's {{as|attack damage}} by half that amount for the same duration.
wiki_detail.leveling2:
{{st|Bonus Attack Damage|{{ap|20 to 40}}}}
{{st|Attack Damage Reduction|{{ap|20/2 to 40/2}}}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* ''Chomp'' will still incur the {{sti|{{as|attack damage}}}} increase for '''Trundle''' even if the attack is {{tip|dodged}}, {{tip|blocked}}, missed while he is {{tip|blind|blinded}}, or it is blocked by {{tip|spell shield}}.
* {{tip|Spell shield}} will only block the attack damage reduction and {{tip|slow}}.

W.name: Frozen Domain
W.description: Trundle turns target location into his domain, gaining Attack Speed, Move Speed, and increased healing from all sources while on it.
W.tooltip: Trundle freezes an area for {{ e4 }} seconds. While inside he gains {{ e1 }}% Move Speed, {{ e2 }}% Attack Speed, and {{ e3 }}% increased healing.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Trundle}} coats the target location in ice for a period. While he is within the area, he gains {{sti|{{as|'''bonus''' attack speed}}}}, {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}}, and increased {{tip|healing}} from all sources.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Trundle''' coats the target location in ice for 8 seconds. While he is within the area, he gains {{sti|{{as|'''bonus''' attack speed}}}}, {{sti|ms|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}}, and 25% increased {{tip|healing}} from all sources.
wiki_detail.leveling:
{{st|Bonus Attack Speed|{{ap|30 to 110}}%}}
{{st|Bonus Movement Speed|{{ap|20 to 52}}%}}
wiki_detail.description2: ''Frozen Domain will cast at max range if cast beyond that.''
wiki_detail.notes: * If ''Frozen Domain'' is attempted to be cast beyond 750 units away, it'll cast at the maximum range instead.<!--This spell is cast type area, 25000 cast range so the "clamping" here is in the script because it's old.-->
_comments: This spell is cast type area, 25000 cast range so the "clamping" here is in the script because it's old.

E.name: Pillar of Ice
E.description: Trundle creates an ice pillar at target location, becoming impassable terrain and slowing all nearby enemy units.
E.tooltip: Trundle creates an icy pillar for {{ e1 }} seconds, briefly Knocking Back enemies directly over it and Slowing nearby enemies by {{ e2 }}%.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Trundle}} erects an ice pillar at the target location that remains for a few seconds, which {{tip|airborne|knocks back}} units hit. The pillar acts as [[terrain]] and {{tip|slows}} nearby enemies.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Trundle''' erects a pillar of ice at the target location for 6 seconds, which {{tip|airborne|knocks back}} units hit to 225 units from its center. The pillar acts as [[terrain]] and {{tip|slows}} nearby enemies.
wiki_detail.effect radius: {{tip|cr|icononly = true}} {{tt|360|Slow zone radius}} / {{tt|225|Knockback radius and destination}}
wiki_detail.custominfo: {{tip|pathing radius|icononly = true}}{{tip|gameplay radius|icononly = true}} {{tt|150|Both pathing and hitbox radius for certain spells that can collide with player-generated terrain}}
wiki_detail.spelleffects:
|notes   = 
* ''Pillar of Ice'' displaces allied units away from the area but does not render them {{tip|airborne}}.
** Allied {{tip|channel|channels}} (e.g. {{si|Recall}}, {{si|Teleport}}) will be {{tip|interrupt|interrupted}} however.
* ''Pillar of Ice'' cannot be placed inside impassable terrain. Attempting to do so will cause the pillar to spawn in the nearest available spot of accessible terrain.
* ''Pillar Of Ice'' triggers effects such as drawing turret aggro, {{ri|Sudden Impact}} and applying {{ii|Elixir of Sorcery}} by dealing 0 {{tip|proc damage|proc}} {{tip|true damage}}.

R.name: Subjugate
R.description: Trundle immediately steals a percent of his target's Health, Armor and Magic Resistance. Over the next 4 seconds the amount of Health, Armor, and Magic Resistance stolen is doubled.
R.tooltip: Trundle drains the lifeforce of an enemy champion, dealing {{ totalpercenthpdamage }} max Health magic damage and stealing {{ armormrshred*100 }}% Armor and Magic Resist over {{ actualdurationofdrainbuff }} seconds.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Trundle}} drains the life force of the target enemy champion, dealing {{as|magic damage}} based on {{as|their '''maximum''' health}} and {{tip|heal|healing}} for the same amount. He also steals a portion of their {{sti|armor penetration|{{as|armor}}}} and {{sti|magic penetration|{{as|magic resist}}}}, and increases in [[size]] while reducing their [[size]].
wiki_detail.blurb2: Half of the damage and stealing is applied instantly, and the other half is applied over a few seconds. The {{as|armor}} and {{as|magic resist}} will remain stolen for a few seconds afterwards.
wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Trundle''' drains the life force out of the target enemy champion, dealing {{as|magic damage}} and {{tip|heal|healing}} himself for the same amount. He also steals 40% of their '''current''' {{sti|armor penetration|{{as|armor}}}} and {{sti|magic penetration|{{as|magic resistance}}}}, and increases in [[size]] by 18% while reducing the target's [[size]] by {{fd|9.9}}%.
wiki_detail.leveling: {{st|Total Magic Damage|{{as|{{ap|20 to 30}}% {{as|(+ 2% per 100 AP)}} of the target's '''maximum''' health|health}}}}
wiki_detail.description2: Half of the '''total''' damage and stealing are applied instantly, and the other half, as well as the size modifiers, are applied every second over the next 4 seconds, even if the target has {{tip|death|died}}.
wiki_detail.leveling2: {{st|Initial Magic Damage|{{as|{{ap|20/2 to 30/2}}% {{as|(+ 1% per 100 AP)}} of the target's '''maximum''' health|health}}|Magic Damage Per Second|{{as|{{ap|20/8 to 30/8}}% {{as|(+ {{fd|0.25}}% per 100 AP)}} of the target's '''maximum''' health|health}}}}
wiki_detail.description3: The {{as|armor}} and {{as|magic resistance}} will remain stolen for 4 seconds after the drain has ended.
wiki_detail.notes:
* The '''total''' value of armor and magic resist stolen and damage dealt is determined at the time of cast. The stolen [[champion statistic|stats]] do not update dynamically relative to the target's stats at any point during the effect, as the steal is applied to the '''current''' value of armor and magic resistance.
* '''Trundle''' gains {{fd|11.7}} extra [[range]] on his {{tip|basic attack|basic attacks}} (relative to his and enemy center) as a consequence of his increased [[size]].
** So do his enemies.
** He gains {{fd|3.78}} to {{fd|6.255}} range against the target he ''subjugated'', depending on their base size, and so does the target against him.
* The debuff on the target also ''persists through death''.
* '''Trundle''' will lose the buff if the target loses the debuff, for example if it enters {{tip|resurrection}}.{{bug|2}}
{{Unit-targeted cancel conditions}}
